data.title: Rails blockbuster: Union Pacific may be looking to acquire CSX or Norfolk Southern
data.description:
Union Pacific Corporation (NYSE:UNP) is weighing a major acquisition, according to SEMFOR. Sources indicate that the company is working with investment bankers at Morgan Stanley.

The report did not reveal the rail giant's target but highlighted that Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) CEO Jim Vena has talked publicly about the virtues of a transcontinental railroad. That could indicate the company is looking at East Coast carriers CSX Corporation (NASDAQ:CSX) or Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC).

A merger would be likely to face major regulatory scrutiny. CSX Corporation (NASDAQ:CSX) has a market cap of over $62 billion, and Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) has a market cap of over $59 billion.

The two largest railroad mergers of all time were the Canadian Pacific-Kansas City Southern combination in 2023 at $31 billion and the Canadian National-Burlington Northern Santa Fe combination in 1999 at $28 billion.

In premarket trading, CSX (CSX) gained 5.4% and Norfolk Southern (NSC) was up 2.6%, while Union Pacific (UNP) was down 2.2%.

data.title: OFG Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.10, revenue of $182.2M beats by $0.52M
data.description:
• OFG Bancorp press release (NYSE:OFG): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $182.2M (+1.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.52M.
  • Net interest margin of 5.31%, return on average assets of 1.73%, return on average tangible common stockholders’ equity of 16.96%, and efficiency ratio of 52.04%.
  • CET1 ratio was 13.99% compared to 14.27% in 1Q25 and 14.29% in 2Q24. Tangible Common Equity ratio was 10.20% compared to 10.30% in 1Q25 and 10.09% in 2Q24. Tangible Book Value per share was $27.67 compared to $26.66 in 1Q25 and $24.18 in 2Q24.

data.title: Main Street Capital estimates $0.98 to $1.00 NII for Q2
data.description:
• Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) sees Net Investment Income (NII) at $0.98 to $1.00 per share for Q2 2025.
  • The distributable net investment income estimate falls within the range of $1.05 to $1.07 per share.
  • The corporation gives preliminary estimate of net asset value (NAV) per share as of June 30, 2025 of $32.27 to $32.33, representing an increase of $0.24 to $0.30 per share, or 0.7% to 0.9%, from the NAV per share of $32.03 as of March 31, 2025. The increase after the impact of the supplemental dividend paid in June 2025 of $0.30 per share.
  • Main Street estimates a quarterly annualized return on equity (ROE) of over 17% for Q2 2025.

data.title: U.S. auto retail: Sonic, Asbury, and Group 1 see downgrades at JPMorgan; Cars.com is upgraded
data.description:
JPMorgan issued a sweeping preview of the U.S. auto retail sector ahead of the Q2 earnings season. Analyst Rajat Gupta said the overall risk-reward profile of the sector is negative after share price outperformance amidst weakening fundamentals. Gupta warned that there is potential for deceleration in EPS growth as supply exceeds demand. The firm issued three downgrades (ABG, GPI, SAH) and one upgrade.

Cars.com (CAR) (upgraded to Overweight from Neutral): "There are signs of improvement in product development and adoption, along with increased optionality following the recent expansion into digital D2D wholesale through the acquisition of DealerClub."

Lithia Motors (NYSE:LAD) (Overweight rating): "LAD is now the only OW rated name in our coverage given room to disconnect favorably on EPS/EBITDA growth from adjacent drivers like DFC, and relatively higher balance sheet optionality with a greater emphasis on buybacks. 

Group 1 Automotive (NYSE:GPI) (downgraded to Neutral from Overweight): "We expect consistent superior execution, though see outsized revision benefits as fading in the medium-term as Inchcape cost out actions are in the rear-view, UK faces a tough backdrop, and there are minimal adjacent earnings drivers."

AutoNation (Neutral): "Shares have outperformed YTD on the back of relatively cleaner revisions, with AN Finance turning profitable sooner than anticipated. The company is also a relativebeneficiary of tariffs given their brand mix, though as an offset, is likely to see mixed fundamentals from outsized exposure to Florida where housing prices are under pressure. That said, like peers they are not immune to industry pressures broadly, and we believe AN Finance inflection is still a couple of years away, as NIMs normalize to current origination spreads, to drive earnings differentiation."

Asbury Automotive Group (NYSE:ABG) (downgraded to Underweight from Neutral): "The company is likely to face a subdued relative revision backdrop, given continued weak SS performance, and minimal balance sheet levers (~3.5x net debt/EBITDA pro-forma for the Herb Chambers acquisition). Brand mix is favorable in context of tariffs, though there remains continued pressure at Stellantis (volumes down ~11% y/y in 2Q vs +2% for industry) and also relatively weaker regional mix (D.C, Florida). "

Sonic Automotive (NYSE:SAH) (downgraded to Underweight from Overweight): "After a period of strong outperformance (~2,600 bps YTD and ~3,000bps last 12 months), largely deserved as franchise execution remains above average and EchoPark has demonstrated a strong return to profitable growth, we believe shares are due for a pull back given relatively unfavorable exposure to brands impacted by auto tariffs, potential for affordability headwinds slowing EchoPark recovery, and tougher comps in P&S in 2H/early 2026."

Penske Automotive Group (NYSE:PAG) (Underweight rating): "While management execution has remained among best-in-class, we continue to see fundamentals as tough given unfavorable exposure to tariff-exposed brands, a tough UK backdrop, and continued weakness in freight markets. While the relative multiple has compressed from the peaks of 2022/2023, we look for a better entry point and/or signs of a turn in the non-auto businesses to get constructive."

data.title: KKR invests in e-commerce platform SupplyHouse, terms undisclosed
data.description:
Investment firm KKR & Co. (NYSE:KKR) has invested in the e-commerce platform for the distribution of HVAC, plumbing, and electrical products, SupplyHouse.

The financial terms of the strategic partnership were not disclosed.

KKR is making its investment through Ascendant Strategy, which invests in middle market businesses.

Shares were -0.12% pre-market to $142.88.
